title: Instructional Resources: Library Materials
description: Conroe ISD's Policy EFB governs the selection, acquisition, and oversight of library materials in district schools. It establishes the roles of the Board, Superintendent, and staff in approving and managing library collections, including rules around donated materials and restrictions on certain content. The policy also outlines a formal process for challenging library materials and describes parental rights related to library resources.

timeline_local.summary: Conroe ISD's Policy EFB (Library Materials) was comprehensively rewritten, removing the previous decentralized, librarian-driven collection development framework and replacing it with a Board-centric approval and oversight model. The updated policy adds explicit prohibitions on pornographic material, requires Superintendent certification of all library materials for compliance, mandates Board approval of all library material acquisitions and donations, establishes a formal statutory challenge process aligned with Texas Education Code §33.027, and strengthens parental rights including opt-out and notification provisions.

description_local: This policy governs the selection, use, and removal of library and instructional materials in the district's schools, including new rules about what types of content are prohibited. It applies to all library books and teaching materials available to students, and establishes clear definitions around sexually explicit or pornographic content that cannot be included in school collections. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it directly shapes what reading and learning materials students can access at school, and it reflects recent state legislation aimed at setting stricter content standards for school libraries.
